app.name: Cairn
setup.title: Welcome to Cairn
setup.subtitle: Create your admin account to get started.
setup.error.password_short: Password must be at least 12 characters.
setup.error.network: Couldn't reach server. Check your connection.
login.title: Sign in to Cairn
login.error.rate_limited: Too many failed attempts. Wait a few minutes.
login.error.network: Couldn't reach server. Check your connection.
login.error.generic: Sign in failed. Please try again.
dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.placeholder: The dashboard is coming soon. In the meantime, use the navigation on the left.
dashboard.add_monitor_desc: Track an HTTP endpoint, port, certificate, or service.
dashboard.create_status_page_desc: Publish a public page showing the health of your monitors.
dashboard.create_component_desc: Components let you organize monitors and roll their status up.
maintenance.title: Maintenance
maintenance.list.title: Maintenance windows
maintenance.list.empty_past: No past maintenance windows in the last 90 days.
maintenance.fields.title: Title
maintenance.fields.description_help: Markdown supported. This appears on the public status page during the maintenance.
maintenance.fields.starts_at_help: If in the past, the maintenance starts immediately.
maintenance.fields.ends_at_help: Auto-completes at this time, or end early via the detail page.
maintenance.fields.affected_required: Pick at least one affected component.
maintenance.fields.no_components: No components yet. Create one first.
maintenance.fields.ends_before_starts: End time must be after start time.
maintenance.fields.locked_in_progress: Cannot be changed once maintenance is in progress.
maintenance.create.title: Schedule maintenance
maintenance.edit.title: Edit maintenance
maintenance.detail.not_found_description: It may have been deleted, or the link is wrong.
maintenance.cancel.confirm_description: Cancel this scheduled maintenance? Subscribers won't be notified.
maintenance.end_now.confirm_description: End this maintenance window now? A 'maintenance ended' notification will be sent to configured channels.
maintenance.delete.confirm_description: This permanently removes the maintenance window. Historical (completed) windows can't be deleted.
errors.forbidden: You don't have permission to do that.
errors.rate_limited: Too many requests. Try again shortly.
errors.internal_error: Something went wrong on the server.
monitors.list.title: Monitors
monitors.list.empty_description: Create your first monitor to start watching a service.
monitors.fields.name: Name
monitors.fields.expected_status_codes_in: Use digits, commas, dashes, and spaces only.
monitors.fields.notification_channels_em: No channels available. Create one under Notifications.
monitors.tooltips.interval: How often Cairn runs this check, in seconds. Smaller intervals catch outages faster but use more resources.
monitors.tooltips.timeout: How long to wait for a response before considering the check failed. Must be less than interval.
monitors.tooltips.retries: If a check fails, retry this many times before recording the failure. Useful for flaky networks.
monitors.tooltips.failure_threshold: How many consecutive failures before Cairn opens an incident. Default 3 means brief network blips don't fire alerts.
monitors.tooltips.recovery_threshold: How many consecutive successes before Cairn auto-resolves an open incident. Default 1 resolves immediately on recovery.
monitors.tooltips.component: Optional: group this monitor under a component shown on your status page.
monitors.tooltips.url: Full URL to check, including https:// or http://.
monitors.tooltips.method: HTTP method. Most checks use GET. Use POST/PUT for write endpoints that should reject malformed requests.
monitors.tooltips.expected_status_codes: HTTP status codes that mean 'up'. Examples: 200, 200-299, 200,301,302, 200-204,301-304.
monitors.tooltips.count: How many ping packets to send per check. More packets give more accurate latency but take longer.
monitors.tooltips.record_type: DNS record type to query. A for IPv4, AAAA for IPv6, CNAME for aliases, MX for mail, TXT for text, NS for name servers.
monitors.tooltips.resolver: DNS server to use, in host:port format. Empty uses the system resolver.
monitors.tooltips.warn_days: Mark check as degraded when the certificate expires within this many days.
monitors.tooltips.critical_days: Mark check as down when the certificate expires within this many days.
monitors.tooltips.dsn: Database connection string. Example for Postgres: postgres://user:password@host:5432/dbname?sslmode=require. Stored encrypted.
monitors.tooltips.query: SQL query to run. Defaults to SELECT 1, which just verifies connectivity.
monitors.tooltips.redis_password: Redis password if AUTH is enabled. Stored encrypted.
monitors.tooltips.grpc_service: Optional service name to check. Empty checks overall server health.
monitors.tooltips.grpc_tls: Use TLS. Required for production gRPC; off only for plaintext testing.
monitors.create.title: New monitor
monitors.edit.title: Edit monitor
monitors.detail.not_found_description: The monitor you're looking for doesn't exist or has been deleted.
monitors.delete.confirm_description: Delete monitor "{name}"? This cannot be undone.
monitors.push.note: A push token will be generated. POST heartbeats to /push/{token}.
components.list.title: Components
components.list.empty_description: No components yet. Group your monitors to organize the public status page.
components.fields.name: Name
components.create.title: New component
components.edit.title: Edit component
components.detail.not_found_description: The component you're looking for doesn't exist or has been deleted.
components.detail.monitors_empty: No monitors linked to this component.
components.attach_monitors.title: Add monitors to {name}
components.attach_monitors.empty: All existing monitors are in this component.
components.attach_monitors.no_monitors: You don't have any monitors yet.
components.delete.confirm_description: Delete component "{name}"? Monitors will lose their component link.
status_pages.list.title: Status pages
status_pages.list.empty_description: No status pages yet. Create one to publish a public status page for your monitors.
status_pages.fields.title: Title
status_pages.fields.custom_footer_help: Allowed tags: a, p, br, strong, em, span. Links open in new tab.
status_pages.fields.is_default_replace: This will replace the current default status page.
status_pages.create.title: New status page
status_pages.edit.title: Edit status page
status_pages.detail.password_set: This page is currently password protected.
status_pages.detail.components_empty: No components on this status page yet.
status_pages.detail.add_component_none: All components are already on this page.
status_pages.detail.not_found_descriptio: The status page you're looking for doesn't exist or has been deleted.
status_pages.delete.confirm_description: Delete status page "{title}"? This cannot be undone.
status_pages.delete.cannot_delete_only: Cannot delete the only status page.
status_pages.footer.title: Footer
status_pages.footer.elements_help: Build the footer from links, text, and separators. Items render in this order.
status_pages.footer.element.label: Label
status_pages.footer.html_help: For advanced users. Allowed tags: a, p, br, strong, em, span. Scripts and unsafe URLs are stripped.
status_pages.footer.error.url_invalid: URL must start with http://, https://, or mailto:
notifications.list.title: Notification channels
notifications.list.empty_description: No notification channels yet. Create one to send alerts when monitors fail.
notifications.fields.name: Name
notifications.fields.extra_headers_help: One per line, format: Name: value
notifications.create.title: New notification channel
notifications.edit.title: Edit channel
notifications.delete.confirm_description: Delete channel "{name}"? Monitors using it will stop sending notifications via this channel.
notifications.detail.not_found_descripti: The channel you're looking for doesn't exist or has been deleted.
incidents.list.title: Incidents
incidents.fields.title: Title
incidents.create.title: New incident
incidents.edit.title: Edit incident
incidents.detail.resolve_confirm_descrip: Mark this incident as resolved? This will notify subscribers.
incidents.detail.affected_empty: No monitors linked to this incident.
incidents.detail.not_found_description: The incident you're looking for doesn't exist or has been deleted.
incidents.delete.confirm_description: Delete incident "{title}"? Updates and links will be removed. This cannot be undone.
settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Manage your account, users, and system preferences.
settings.backup.title: Backup
settings.backup.subtitle: Download a copy of the database, optionally bundled with the encryption key.
settings.backup.mode_db_only_help: Just the SQLite database. Back up your CAIRN_ENCRYPTION_KEY separately.
settings.backup.mode_bundle_encrypted_he: Database + encryption key, protected by a passphrase. Use this if you want a single backup file.
settings.backup.mode_bundle_plain_help: Database + encryption key in a plain archive. Anyone with this file can read all secrets.
settings.backup.passphrase_help: At least 12 characters. There is no way to recover the bundle without it.
settings.backup.passphrase_too_short: Passphrase must be at least 12 characters.
settings.backup.plain_warning_body: This bundle will include your encryption key in plain text. Anyone with the file can read all stored secrets.
settings.backup.plain_warning_checkbox: I understand this bundle is unencrypted and anyone with it can read all secrets.
settings.backup.restore_intro: Restore is performed via the CLI for safety. Stop Cairn, then run:
settings.backup.restore_encrypted_note: If your backup is an encrypted bundle, you'll be prompted for the passphrase.
settings.profile.section_info_help: Update your display name and email. Username is fixed.
settings.profile.section_password_help: Use at least 12 characters. Other sessions will be signed out.
settings.profile.password_too_short: Password must be at least 12 characters.
settings.users.title: Users
settings.users.subtitle: Manage user accounts and roles.
settings.users.delete_confirm: Delete user "{username}"? This cannot be undone.
settings.users.delete_self_blocked: You cannot delete your own account.
settings.system.section_incidents_help: Format used when displaying incident IDs.
settings.system.section_reopen_help: Defaults applied when a monitor recovers and fails again. Per-monitor overrides take precedence.
settings.system.reopen_window_help: If a previously resolved incident's monitor fails again within this many seconds, reopen it. 0 disables.
settings.system.reopen_mode_always_help: Reopen any matching incident within the window.
settings.system.reopen_mode_flapping_hel: Reopen only when the monitor flaps within the window.
settings.retention.section_help: How long to keep raw, hourly, and daily check results.
settings.retention.raw_days_help: Per-check raw results. Must be ≤ hourly.
settings.retention.hourly_days_help: Hourly aggregates. Must be ≤ daily unless 'keep daily forever' is on.
settings.retention.keep_forever_help: Daily rollups are tiny — safe to keep indefinitely.
settings.retention.must_be_positive: All retention periods must be at least 1 day.
settings.retention.raw_le_hourly: Raw retention must be ≤ hourly retention.
settings.retention.hourly_le_daily: Hourly retention must be ≤ daily retention.
settings.about.tagline: Self-hosted uptime monitoring, incidents, and status pages.
settings.about.privacy_body: No telemetry, no phone-home, no external requests beyond what you configure.
monitors.advanced.title: Advanced
monitors.advanced.reopen_help: Per-monitor overrides for incident reopen behavior. Leave off to use system defaults.
login.2fa_help: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app, or one of your recovery codes.
login.error.challenge_expired: Login expired. Please sign in again.
settings.users.reset_2fa_confirm: Disable 2FA for "{name}"? They will be able to sign in with just their password until they re-enroll.
settings.users.force_logout: Other sessions for this user will be signed out.
settings.users.cannot_change_own_role: You cannot change your own role.
auth.2fa.disabled_help: Add an extra layer of security by requiring a code from your authenticator app at sign-in.
auth.2fa.enroll_help: Scan the QR code with your authenticator app, or enter the setup key manually. Then enter the 6-digit code to confirm.
auth.2fa.codes_save_now: Save these recovery codes somewhere safe. Each one can be used once if you lose access to your authenticator. They will not be shown again.
auth.2fa.codes_acknowledge: I have saved these recovery codes.
auth.2fa.disable_help: Confirm your password and a current code to disable 2FA on your account.
auth.2fa.regenerate_help: Existing recovery codes will stop working. You will receive 10 new codes.
